lines: Ruth stands near the chalk drawing, trying not to look proud that it survived.
"Micah says I cannot touch the ball, and being right about it was rude of him. Watching still counts."

choices.label: Tell her Micah has the ball back

lines: Ruth scuffs one shoe over the dust.
"I only kicked it because Micah said I could not hit the far wall; I hit much farther than that."
"I said sorry, but he says sorry does not roll back."

choices.label: Ask if she heard anything below
Tell her Micah has the ball back

lines: "A proper kick needs room. Catacombs have too much room in the wrong direction."
"If Micah writes a law about it, I want a trial. Susanna can be judge. She already looks like one."
"He has it back? Good. My stomach was doing the thing where it tries to climb out."
"If he asks, I was sorry first and proud second. The order matters."
"The wall moved, and dust made my foot ambitious."
"Do not make that face. I wanted a trial, not a promise I would win."
"Micah worries like grown people do, only smaller and louder."
"He forgives if you wait long enough, or if you find the exact right rock. I am looking for the rock."
"I drew what I dreamed. Susanna said not to show the little ones. I said I am one of the little ones, and she made the tired face."
"A round thing hung in the dark, like a coin but cold. I do not know why it was falling."
"I can draw turnips instead. Turnips never fall from the sky unless Ruth is very angry."
"The lower hall clicks sometimes, not like teeth. More like someone tapping a cup with one nail."
"I told Micah: pipes. He said pipes do not wait until everyone is quiet."
"Yes."
"But Joanna says scared people can still hold bandages, and Ruth says scared people can still peel beans. I am waiting for someone to need a bean peeled."
